summary:
Acupotomy is not used to treat muscle stiffness; it is an invasive procedure primarily intended for controlling bleeding, not addressing muscular dysfunction. Evidence supports acupuncture or dry needling—distinct procedures—for alleviating muscle stiffness through improved circulation and reduced spasticity.  

Final answer: B – no.
